sources: Avoma call 6 May 2025 (Project Sapay - Vendor Proposal)
Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zee, Carl and regional Zycus lead)
metrics.narrative: The commercial scope was quantified in detail during the 6 May 2025 clarification call: a 5-year term covering roughly 30,000 invoices, 250 annual AI-scored contracts, and unlimited supplier-portal access with AI-based risk scoring beyond the initial 500. Jennelyn Nicolas formally weighted the evaluation 50% functional fit and 50% technical capability/ROI on 7 Apr 2025, and Zeeshan Pervez has directly articulated the business pain driving the case: roughly 70% of ACEN's spend (non-PO payments like loan and tax) is disconnected from their current system (28 Feb 2025 call).

champion.narrative: Zeeshan Pervez behaves as an internal champion — confirming CFO alignment, personally pushing his own team, and repeatedly advocating for Zycus internally ('this is something... I'll probably be more interested in,' 28 Feb 2025) — and a 13 Feb 2025 note separately names the CPO-level contact as a confirmed champion. But that advocacy has not converted into released budget across three deferrals, and the champion thread went quiet for months (only rep-side pings on 16 Mar 2026 and 4 May 2026) until the buyer replied 7 Jul 2026. Champion strength is developing and currently at risk.

competition.narrative: Zycus was confirmed shortlisted to the final two against SAP Ariba on 3 Jun 2025, and the SAP secondary integration/connection fee is being used almost as a competitive lever (26 May 2025 note describes it as SAP 'playing this low integrity card of charging a fee to integrate Zycus'). Coupa, GEP Worldwide and Ivalua sit in the Salesforce Competitors field but never surface in any call evidence, consistent with being eliminated earlier in the RFP long-list before the 3 Jun 2025 down-select to two.

sources: Avoma call 21 Feb 2025 (Zycus System - Full Demo)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well documented and buyer-voiced: the in-house-built P2P system is emotionally tied to the team that built it (28 Feb 2025 call), roughly 70% of spend (non-PO payments such as loan and tax) is manually disconnected from the system, supplier onboarding runs manually via email with third-party verification, and ADB funding is imposing ESG/supplier code-of-conduct requirements the current stack can't meet. Zeeshan Pervez personally framed the non-PO payment gap as something his CFO 'is going to be very laser sharp on.'

paper_process.narrative: Zycus proactively sent the MSA and Order Form to Jeff De Sagun for preliminary legal review on 13 Aug 2025, specifically to get ahead of legal turnaround time before commercials were finalized. No redline, legal feedback, or sign-off status has been confirmed in any record since — this is an open thread roughly 11 months old.

sources: Salesforce Contact Role: Jonathan Back, Executive Sponsor
economic_buyer.narrative: Jonathan Back (CFO and Chief Strategy Officer, Executive Sponsor) was named and personally engaged once, on the 28 Feb 2025 call, where he was described by Zeeshan Pervez as one of only four people who will make the final decision and as actively pushing internally ('why haven't we moved this'). No direct Zycus contact with Jonathan Back has been logged in any source since that single call, over 16 months ago — access to this economic buyer is real but entirely indirect, routed through Zeeshan Pervez.

decision_process.narrative: The process ran RFP (Mar 2025) to clarification to onsite POC (11 Apr 2025) to proposal to commercial clarification (May-Jun 2025) to a full architecture walkthrough (13 Aug 2025) to references and preliminary legal review, feeding an internal 'evaluation scoring exercise' meant to support an IT steering committee/exco decision. On the 13 Aug 2025 call Jeff De Sagun could not confirm when that steering-committee presentation would happen ('we don't know yet'), and the process has since stalled through three budget-driven exco deferrals.

decision_criteria.narrative: Jennelyn Nicolas supplied a formal weighting on 7 Apr 2025 (50% functional fit, 50% technical capability and ROI), and detailed clarification rounds through May-Aug 2025 worked through specific criteria: user/buyer counts, supplier and risk-module scope, AI-based contract scoring, and the budget-checking approach (Zycus e-procurement vs. ERP).

items.summary: Carl Kimball (Zycus VP APAC) reached out to Zee after a gap in contact, inviting him and colleagues to Zycus' "Horizon" event in Singapore (21-22 July), offering waived fees and a possible speaking slot; this is relationship/brand touchpoint rather than deal progression, and a reply is pending on whether Zee wants to attend.

day_summary.overall: The only activity today was an inbound invite from Zycus' APAC VP inviting Zee to their July industry event in Singapore, positioned as a networking/thought-leadership forum rather than a sales pitch. It's a relationship-nurturing outreach after a period of quiet on the account, not a substantive deal move — the ball is now in Zee's court to respond if interested.

cro_panel.intro: ACEN S2P built a genuinely deep, CFO-backed case through mid-2025 (MSA/Order Form already with the buyer's legal reviewer, shortlisted to the final two against SAP Ariba, onsite POC completed), but the exco budget release it has always depended on slipped three times from September 2025 onward, leaving the account effectively dark until a real buyer reply landed two days ago

confidence_signals.summary: The account cooled hard from Sep 2025 through spring 2026 amid three exco deferrals, but a genuine two-way buyer reply on 7 Jul 2026 (2 days ago) is the first live signal in roughly ten months.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2024-10-08 (~21 months ago). Down-selected to top-2 finalist 2025-06-03; entered/held Vendor Selected since roughly mid-2025 (~13 months in stage). Two exco postponements logged 2025-09-22 and 2025-10-30. One verified two-way buyer reply on 2026-03-24 (105 days before this sweep); rep outreach logged 2026-03-16 and 2026-05-04, with a further rep touch flagged around 2026-07-09. Close date sits far out at 2027-01-31 (208 days), consistent with a rolled-forward placeholder date rather than a confirmed path. Best Case forecast is not supported by any confirmed near-term milestone.

north_star_verdict.headline: Down-selected to a final two against SAP Ariba by Jun 2025, but the deal has been frozen since ACEN's exco twice postponed the project for budget reasons (Sep and Oct 2025); re-engagement since Mar 2026 has been almost entirely rep-initiated.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (downgrade from Best Case until a re-scoped proposal and exco date are confirmed)

flags: Re-confirm the SAP integration-cost resolution and refresh the TCO comparison directly with Zee before the next exco cycle, since that unresolved fee is the lever most likely to tip the top-2 decision toward SAP Ariba (Next Step, 22 May-23 Jun 2025).
Convert the May 2026 Agentic AI/Globe angle into a concrete, budget-fit re-scoped proposal Zee can carry into exco, closing the loop Carl opened in Oct 2025 to trim scope and revise numbers.

Single-threaded reliance on Zeeshan Pervez for exco advancement — no evidence of direct Zycus engagement with Jonathan Back (CFO) or Eric Francia (CEO) in over a year; broaden the thread now that contact has resumed.

The deal has been effectively stalled for roughly ten months since the Sep 2025 postponement; without a dated re-engagement plan anchored to this week's reply, it risks reverting to the in-house P2P status quo or losing further ground to SAP Ariba.
Convert the 7 Jul 2026 buyer reply into a firm next meeting and a confirmed exco/steering-committee date — the account currently has no scheduled forward motion on record.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal's qualification depth is real; the gap is entirely in converting internal buyer advocacy into a released budget and closing the one open competitive cost objection.

customer_preference.evidence: Zeeshan Pervez voiced a clear technical preference for Zycus in the 28 Feb 2025 call ('I've seen this product... I'll probably be more interested in '), citing prior Zycus experience and the intake-management/GenAI differentiator, but this is the last direct buyer-voiced preference statement on record (over 16 months old) and the content of the 7 Jul 2026 reply is not yet known, so this should not be read as a fresh, current preference signal.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus was down-selected to the final two against SAP Ariba by June 2025. The live threat is not really SAP Ariba's product but the fee SAP charges to integrate a third-party tool alongside its own ERP, which the buyer has been actively negotiating directly with SAP since mid-2025. Coupa, GEP Worldwide and Ivalua appear only as early-stage Salesforce field entries with no supporting call evidence, consistent with elimination before the shortlist. The in-house P2P status quo (do-nothing) is arguably the deal's biggest ongoing threat given the repeated exco deferrals.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: ACEN S2P built a genuinely deep, CFO-backed case through mid-2025 (MSA/Order Form already with the buyer's legal reviewer, shortlisted to the final two against SAP Ariba, onsite POC completed), but the exco budget release it has always depended on slipped three times from September 2025 onward, leaving the account effectively dark until a real buyer reply landed two days ago.

deal_risk.text: Budget/exco approval is the single blocking dependency — Zeeshan Pervez needs exco sign-off within a workable budget envelope and has not secured it after three deferrals (22 Sep 2025, 30 Oct 2025, and an implied further slip past the 'end of Jan' 2026 target).

The SAP secondary integration fee is a live, unresolved cost objection (raised 22 May 2025, being negotiated directly with SAP by Jeff De Sagun and Jennelyn Nicolas as of 23 Jun 2025) that could tip the buyer's TCO math toward SAP Ariba if it stays open.

Customer references (BDO/SM) requested 13 Aug 2025 and the revised commercial proposal committed to on 11 Sep 2025 both have no confirmed completion on record, leaving open MEDDPICC gaps heading into any final decision.

win_position.text: Deep qualification history — a strong face-to-face with the extended management team on 13 Feb 2025, CFO Jonathan Back personally backing the project on 28 Feb 2025 ('why haven't we moved this'), and the MSA/Order Form already sent for preliminary legal review on 13 Aug 2025.
Shortlisted to the final two against SAP Ariba (confirmed 3 Jun 2025) on a favorable, buyer-defined criteria split (50% functional fit / 50% tech+ROI, set 7 Apr 2025).

But the deal sat effectively frozen for most of a year: exco postponed the initiative a quarter on 22 Sep 2025 and again on 30 Oct 2025, and the only Next Step activity between Jan 2026 and Jul 2026 was one-way rep outreach (16 Mar 2026, 4 May 2026) with no logged buyer response until 7 Jul 2026.
No direct engagement with the economic sponsor, CFO Jonathan Back, has been logged since the single 28 Feb 2025 call — over 16 months without another confirmed touch at that level.

deal_momentum.text: The account was essentially dark from the Sep 2025 postponement through spring 2026: an intro email on 16 Mar 2026 and a lunch/Agentic-AI outreach on 4 May 2026 both went unanswered in the record.
The one live signal: the buyer replied by email on 7 Jul 2026 (two days ago), the first confirmed two-way contact in roughly ten months, and the rep followed up again today, 9 Jul 2026.
Momentum has not yet been rebuilt beyond that single reply — no new meeting, no confirmed exco date, and no resolution of the SAP integration-cost objection is on record.

customer_commitment.text: Zeeshan Pervez has repeatedly told Zycus his leadership team is aligned (28 Feb 2025: 'you've already got two people kind of aligned on this') and has personally advocated internally.
That advocacy has not converted into a released budget across three deferrals, and the customer reference calls (BDO, SM) Jeff De Sagun requested on 13 Aug 2025 have no confirmed completion since.
